name: run-with-env
description: Run a command with environment variables from a config file or inline --env flags, or read config values with --get/--all, replacing inline VAR=val prefix chains and cat+grep env-read steps.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Command to run with loaded environment (omit for --get/--all modes)

parameters.name: --config
parameters.description: Path to env config file with KEY=VALUE lines

parameters.name: --cd
parameters.description: Change to directory before running command

parameters.name: --env
parameters.description: Set env var as KEY=VALUE (repeatable, replaces inline VAR=val prefix)

parameters.name: --get
parameters.description: Read a single KEY value from config file (replaces env-get KEY)

parameters.name: --all
parameters.description: List all key-value pairs from config file (replaces env-get --all)

parameters.name: --value-only
parameters.description: With --get, output only the value (replaces cut -d= -f2 chains)

parameters.name: --strip-comments
parameters.description: With --all, remove comment lines and blank lines

parameters.name: --head
parameters.description: Show first N lines of command output (replaces | head -N pipe)

parameters.name: --tail
parameters.description: Show last N lines of command output (replaces | tail -N pipe)

examples.expected: Script output with env vars set

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses cd + inline VAR=val prefix chains and cat+grep env-read steps into one command; --head/--tail collapses | head/tail pipes saving output token cost.
